title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: New photo of Rosalie Miller released to seek new witnesses in her 1997 unsolved murder case.
Miller was last seen in December 1996 and found dead from strangulation in January 1997 in Auburn, NH.
Authorities hope the photo jogs memories; public urged to contact NH Cold Case Unit with any information.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, charged with murder after allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island on Thursday night.
Sousa fled in a white Infiniti, later crashing in Swansea, Massachusetts, killing two people in a separate vehicle.
Police found a pistol and .22 caliber ammunition in Sousa's car; he remains hospitalized but is expected to survive.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney considers adding St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas before NHL trade deadline.
Robert Thomas has exceeded 80 points in each of the last two seasons, being an effective playmaker and center.
Acquiring Thomas might require Bruins to part with multiple first-round picks and top prospects.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: A New York couple was fined $3,000 by Maine's Board of Pesticides Control for killing a neighbor's trees.
Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett did not admit guilt for the tree deaths, despite paying the settlement fine.
The late Ruth Graham's family proposed additional reparations, including donations and a public apology, which were not addressed.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Texas A&M WR KC Concepcion impressed with route-running at the NFL Combine, highlighting strong depth at Patriots' wide receiver need.
EDGE players Dani Dennis-Sutton and Malachi Lawrence excelled athletically at the Combine and are projected as Day 2 picks.
Cincinnati WR Jeff Caldwell showcased exceptional athleticism, potentially boosting his draft stock with a fourth-fastest 4.31s 40-yard dash.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots unlikely to make big trades this offseason, avoiding large draft-pick exchanges similar to Micah Parsons deal.
Interest reported in trades for Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby, but asking prices remain high.
Patriots focus on building through the draft, holding 11 picks primarily in later rounds, and $40 million cap space.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power approved to build Massachusetts' largest battery storage on former ExxonMobil site in Everett.
The storage facility spans 16.5 acres and includes 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures with cooling and heating systems.
Part of the Docklands project, the site will integrate renewable energy and connect with existing substations.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on Feb. 28, 2026, joined by musical guest Mumford & Sons.
'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams appeared in a skating sketch and introduced Mumford & Sons with Storrie.
US Olympic hockey team members, including Megan Keller and Hilary Knight, appeared with their gold medals.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Revolution lost 1-0 to New York Red Bulls, marking their second defeat to start the 2026 season.
Brayan Ceballos and Mamadou Fofana returned to strengthen the Revolution's defense against New York's dominant first-half possession.
New England struggled to capitalize on second-half opportunities, with attacking players failing to break through the Red Bulls' defense.

title: Triston Casas reportedly unlikely to be ready by Opening Day, while Kutter Crawford, Patrick Sandoval take step forward in rehab
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Triston Casas is unlikely to return to MLB action by Opening Day after tearing his patellar tendon last May.
Pitchers Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval recently participated in live batting practice at Fenway South during rehab.
Crawford and Sandoval are hopeful, but not certain, about being ready for the Red Sox Opening Day roster.
